project: Wuxia Life P0 Core Experience Remediation
description: Fix the P0 gameplay and correctness issues identified in the improvement roadmap: remove production debug UI, make multi-outcome choice conditions real, suppress event repetition with measurable validation, and unify the main game state source through small approved stories.

userStories.title: Remove Production Debug Blocks
userStories.description: As a player, I want the main game screen to show only game content so that debug fields do not break immersion.
acceptanceCriteria: The main game screen no longer displays debug blocks for currentNode, choices, or lastOutcomeText
Choice, continue, and event outcome display behavior remains available
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Preserve Developer Diagnostics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want diagnostic information to remain available through an explicit debug entry so that UI cleanup does not reduce troubleshooting ability.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostics are shown only through an explicit debug panel or debug entry
The default gameplay flow does not show the diagnostic panel
The debug toggle behavior is unchanged or clearer than before

userStories.title: Record Main Flow UI Validation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want reproducible validation steps for P0 UI cleanup so that production debug text does not return unnoticed.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation covers at least two key nodes from new game, choice event, continue event, and ending flow
Validation steps are documented in a P0 delivery note or test output
Validation documentation contains no local absolute paths

userStories.title: Unify Multi-Outcome Condition Evaluation
userStories.description: As a player, I want multi-outcome choices to resolve according to real conditions so that choices produce trustworthy results.
acceptanceCriteria: Expression conditions are no longer treated as true by default
Outcome condition evaluation reuses the core condition evaluator or an equivalent controlled entry
Unconditional outcomes can still act as fallback outcomes
Unparseable conditions do not silently select an incorrect outcome

userStories.title: Test Multi-Outcome Branches
userStories.description: As a developer, I want multi-outcome branch tests so that condition regressions are caught by the default gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover condition success, condition failure, fallback branch, and branch order priority
At least one test uses an expression condition
npm test passes

userStories.title: Validate AutoResolve Choice Conditions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want autoResolve choices to respect the same availability and outcome rules as manual choices so that automatic events do not bypass conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: autoResolve selects only available choices
autoResolve outcome branches use real condition evaluation
Failures produce readable diagnostics instead of being silently swallowed

userStories.title: Create Event Repetition Reproduction
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a stable reproduction for repeated injury, illness, and economy events so that fixes are grounded in evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: A fixed seed or fixed simulation input reproduces at least one repetition issue
The reproduction report lists adjacent repeated events or repeated event classes
The reproduction command can be run locally

userStories.title: Define Event Repetition Rules
userStories.description: As a designer, I want clear repetition definitions so that suppression rules do not damage legitimate story progression.
acceptanceCriteria: The rules distinguish same-event repetition, same-class repetition, and same-storyline repetition
Allowed and disallowed repeat boundaries are documented
The rules cover injury, illness, economy, romance, identity, main story, and daily events

userStories.title: Implement Basic Repetition Suppression
userStories.description: As a player, I want short-term meaningless event repetition to be suppressed so that the life simulation feels like a story instead of a repeating event table.
acceptanceCriteria: Same events honor maxTriggers and cooldown consistently
High-negative same-class events have short-term suppression or weight decay
Daily event repetition rules are distinct from formal story event rules
Mandatory mainline and critical events are not blocked by the suppression strategy

userStories.title: Measure Event Repetition Rate
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want repetition metrics in simulation output so that future content changes cannot quietly reintroduce repetition problems.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation output includes adjacent repetition statistics
Simulation output includes short-window same-class repetition statistics
Simulation output identifies the most repeated events
P0 repetition thresholds are documented

userStories.title: Audit Current State Sources
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a state-source audit before migration so that unifying state does not accidentally break live flows.
acceptanceCriteria: The audit lists legacy store, new composable, global game engine, save manager, debug panel, and demo component relationships
Each entry is marked as main flow, demo flow, deprecated flow, or needs confirmation
No business code is changed as part of the audit story

userStories.title: Choose the Single Main State Source
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an explicit state-source decision so that future fixes use one state model.
acceptanceCriteria: The single main state source is named
Allowed read-only adapters are named
Deprecated entry points are named
The decision is recorded in the P0 delivery notes

userStories.title: Migrate Main Flow off Legacy State
userStories.description: As a player, I want start, gameplay, ending, and restart flows to read one consistent state so that the UI and engine show the same life.
acceptanceCriteria: The main flow no longer depends on the legacy state source
The ending screen reads state consistent with the game engine
Restarting clears the main state and event state consistently
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Isolate or Remove Deprecated State Links
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want deprecated state links isolated or removed so that future code does not accidentally use old paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Deprecated state entry points have no main-flow references
Any retained legacy entry is clearly marked as demo or historical compatibility
No unused imports or obvious dead links remain from the migration
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Add State Consistency Regression Coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want regression coverage after state unification so that save, ending, and restart flows do not split state again.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover key state consistency across new game, event progress, choice handling, ending, or restart
Failures point to state synchronization problems
npm test passes

userStories.title: Close P0 with Fresh Verification Evidence
userStories.description: As a project owner, I want a P0 delivery summary with fresh verification evidence so that the project can decide whether to move into P1.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run typecheck passes
npm run build passes
npm test passes
The P0 delivery summary includes changed files, verification results, residual risks, and follow-up recommendations
